and therefore shall perish in the gainsay of Corah . this be the doctrine of God , therefore saint Paul exhort we not to rebel , nor to speak evil of our king , 1 time 2.2 . be they what they will ; but first of all , or before all thing , to make prayer and supplication for our king , and for all that be in authority . and i wonder what spirit , except it be the spirit of hell it self , dare ever presume to answer and evade such plain and pregnant place of scripture , to countenance disobedience , and to justify their rebellion : and therefore , 2. 2. by the doctrine of the church . the church of Christ believe this doctrine to be the truth of God ; for , as God be the supreme lord of all , which judge all , and be judge of none ; so the king and prince of the earth , which do correct and judge other , be to be correct and judge of none , but only of God , to who power and authority they be only subject ; and therefore king David , understand his own station well enough , when he be both a adulterer and a murderer , and pray to God for mercy , say , against thou only have i sin ; because i acknowledge none other my superior on earth beside thou alone ; and i have no judge beside thou which can call i to examination , or inflict any punishment on i for my transgression : and so the poet say , regum timendorum in proprio greges , Reges in ipsos imperium est jovis . but you will object against saint Cyril , if it be impiety to say unto the king , thou do amiss ; how shall we excuse Samuel that tell king Saul , he do foolish ; and Nathan that reprove king David , and Elias that say to king Ahab , it be he and his father house that make Israel to sin ; and John Baptist that tell Herod , it be not lawful for he to have his brother wife . i answer , 1. that by the mouth of these man God himself reprove they ; because these man be no private person , what the priest or prophet may do , private man may not do . but extraordinary inspire with the spirit of God , to perform the extraordinary message of God . 2. i say as i say before , that as Moses may correct and punish Aaron if he do amiss , so Aaron the priest inregard of his call , may reprove and admonish Moses the chief magistrate when he do offend ; but so , that he do it wise , and with that love and reverence which he owe unto Moses , as to his god ; not public to disgrace and vilify his prince unto his people , but modest and private to amend his fault , and reconcile he to God : and this be the work of his office which he ought to do as he be a priest , and not of his person , which ought not to do it , as he be his subject . 3. reason it self confirm this truth , 3. by humane reason . because the king be the head of the body politic ; and the member can neither judge the head , because they be subject unto it ; nor cut it off , because than they kill themselves , and cease to be the member of that head : and therefore the subject with no reason can either judge or depose their king . 4. 4. from the welware of every commonwealth . the event of every war be doubtful . the public safety and welfare of any commonwealth require that the subject shall never rebel against their king . 1. because the event of a rebellious war be both dubious and dangerous ; CHAP. vi . of combination or consotiation of church into class or synod . question 1. whither be it lawful for particular congregation , consist of elder and brethren , and walk with a right foot in the truth and peace of the gospel , to join together in consotiation on combination of church ? Answ. though the church of a particular congregation , consist of elder and brethren , mr. cotton . etc be the first subject of all church power needful to be exercise within it self , and consequent be independent from any other church or synod in the use of it ; yet it be lawful , yea it be a useful , safe and wholesome , and holy ordinance of Christ , and in some case necessary for such particular church to join together in holy covenant or communion and consotiation ( so i think it be intend by the author , though the print copy say consolation ) among themselves to administer all their church affair which be of weighty and difficult and common concernment , not without common consultation and consent of other church about they . mr. Hookers survey of discipline , preface A. 3. mr. cotton of the key page . 54. line 27. quest . 2. why do some say this consotiation of church be , useful ? Answ. the consotiation of church be not only lawful but useful , serve not alone to search out the truth , but to settle the heart of all that be sincere mind in a right apprehension of the same . mr. Hooker . mr. Hooker survey of discipline , part 4. page . 1. line 2. when many , and those select and eminent , lay the best of their ability together , improve their part and prayer , disquisition , consultation , determination to promote the knowledge and practice of the thing of Christ , the establishment of the church in the unity of the faith and their eternal peace . mr. Hooker survey of discipline , part 4. page . 2. line 2. quest . 3. why do you say it be safe ? Answ. it be safe ; for in the multitude of councillor there be safety , as in civil so in church affair , Prov. 11.14 . mr. cotton key page . 55. line 4. mr. cotton . the light of nature and right reason force man out of their own necessity and experience to confess this that in multitude of counseller there be safety , et plus vident oculi quam oculus , etc the issue of the council evidence as much , act 16.4 , 5. they deliver the decree , etc and the church be establish in faith . mr. Hookers survey of discipline , part 4. page . 2. line 9 quest . 4. but if the church be of a good and strong constitution and walk with a right foot in the truth and peace of the gospel , what need of such consotiation ? Answ. though this or that particular church may be of a good and strong constitution , and walk with a right foot in the truth and peace of the gospel ; mr. Cotton , yet all church be not in a like athletic plight , and they that be not so , will be loath to call in , or look out for help as much or more than other , though they have more need than other ; yea and the best church may soon degenerate and stand in as much need of help as other , and for want of it may sink and fall into a deep apostasy , which other church may have prevent if they have discern it at first . mr. Cotton Keyes page . 55. line 7. quest . 5. why do you say this combination and consotiation of church be wholesome ? Answ. it be wholesome as tend to maintain brotherly love and soundness of doctrine in church , mr. cotton . and to prevent many offence which may grow up in this or that particular church , when it transact all such thing in it self without con-mr . cotton of the key p. 55. line . 17. quest . 6. but how do you prove it a ordinance of Christ ? Answ. it be a holy ordinance of Christ as have just warrant from a like precedent ; the apostle be as much independent one from another , mr. cotton . and stand in as little need of help one from another , as church do . and yet Paul go up to Jerusalem to confer with Peter , Jam•s and John , lest he shall in vain run in the course of his ministry , Gal. 2.2 . and though in conference the chief apostle add nothing to Paul , ver . 6. yet when they perceive the gospel of uncircumcision be commit to Paul and Barnabas , as that of the circumcision be to Peter , James and John , they give unto one another the right hand of fellowship . now than it will follow by just proportion , that if the Apostl s who be independent each of they one of another , have need to consult and confer together , to procure a free passage to their call , and to their doctrine ; than sure church , and elder of church , though independent one of another , have need to communicate te their course and proceed in such case one with another , to procure the free passage to the same . and if the apostle give the right hand of fellowship one to another , do mutual strengthen their hand in the work of the ministry ; then the elder of Churches give the right hand of fellowship one to another in their ordination , or upon any fit occasion , can but much strengthen the heart and hand of one another in the lord work . mr. cotton of the key . page 55. line 22. quest . 7. be there any thing which may be add , if not for confirmation , yet for illustration of the point . Answ. yes , by compare the dimension of the new Jerusalem , which be the perfect platform of a pure church , as it shall be constitute in the jewish church state at their last conversion . mr. cotton . the dimension of this church , as they be describe by Ezekiel , chapter 48.30 . be twelve furlong , that be , about three mile in length , and as many in breadth . but the dimension of the same church , rev. 21.16 . be say to be twelve thousand furlong . now how can those two dimension stand together , that be so far discrepant one from another , as twelve , and twelve thousand ? the fit and fair reconciliation seem plain to be this ; that Ezekiel speak of the dimension of any ordinary jewish church of one particular congregation , but John speak of the dimension of many particular jewish church combine together in some case , even to the communion of a thousand church . mr. cotton of the key . page 56. line 7. object . but Theologia symbolica non est argumentativa ; argument from parable and mystical resemblance be not valid . Answ. if there be no argumentative power in parable , mr. cotton . why do the lord Jesus delight so much in that kind of teach ? and why do John and Daniel , and Ezekiel , deliver a great part of their prophecy in parable , if we must take they for riddle , and not for document or argument ? sure if they serve not for argument , they serve not for document . mr. cotton of the key . page 56. line 36. quest . 8. but be this combination of church necessary , as well as lawful ? Answ. the consociation of church be not only lawful , mr. Hooker . but in some case necessary . mr. Hookers survey of discipline . preface . a. 3. Answ. because it be difficult , if not impossible , for many church to come altogether in one place , platform of discipline . in all their member universal ; therefore they may assemble by their delegate or messenger , as the church of Antioch go not all to Jerusalem , but some select man for that purpose ; because none be , or shall be more fit to know the state of the church , nor to advise of way for the good thereof than Elders ; therefore it be fit that in the choice of the messenger for such assembly , they have special respect unto such ; yet , in as much as not only Paul and Barnabas , but certain other also be send to Jerusalem from Antioch , act 15. and when they be come to Jerusalem , not only the apostle and elder , but other brethren also do assemble and meet about the matter , therefore synod be to consist both of Elders and other church-member , endue with gift , and send by the church , not exclude the presence of any brethren in the church . platform of discipline chapter 16. sect . 6. quest . 12. whether may there be call and k•pt a national synod or no ? Answ. it may so fall out that the state of all the church in the country may be corrupt , mr. cotton . and beginning to discern their corruption , may desire the concourse and counsel of one another for a speedy and safe , and general reformation ; and then so meet , all confer together , may renew their covenant with God , and conclude and determine upon a course that may tend to the heal and salvation of they all : this be frequent in the old testament in the time of Asa , Hezekiah and Josiah ; these and the like example be not peculiar to the Israelite as one entire national church ; these example hold forth no superiority in one church or court over another , but all of they in a equal manner give advice in common , and therefore such example be fit precedent for church of equal power within themselves , to assemble together and take order with one accord for the reformation of they all . mr. cotton of the key . page . 24 l. 22. quest . 13. but what be the work that proper belong to synod ?
